paper_map.title: High Temperature Oxidation of Iron-Manganese-Aluminum Based Alloys
paper_map.abstract_summary: Isothermal oxidation of high purity Fe–Mn–Al alloys containing 0-40% Mn and 0-15% Al was studied at 600, 800, and 1000°C in 200 torr oxygen. Scale morphology depended primarily on Mn and Al contents, with four distinct scale types mapped on an 800°C oxide map. Alloys with 5-10% Mn and 6-10% Al formed continuous protective alumina scales and exhibited maximum oxidation resistance, while austenite promoted breakdown and chromium additions reduced critical Al content.
paper_map.research_objective: To determine the isothermal oxidation behavior and scale evolution mechanisms in Fe–Mn–Al alloys as a function of Mn and Al contents, and to identify the compositional domains yielding protective Al2O3 scales with or without chromium additions.
material_systems: Fe–Mn–Al–Cr quaternary alloys (e.g., Fe–10Mn–4Al–5Cr, Fe–15Mn–5Al–5Cr, Fe–7.5Mn–4Al–5Cr)
Ferritic (α), austenitic (γ), and duplex (α+γ) phase regions
main_process_variables: Oxidation time: 24 h, selected up to 100 h
Atmosphere: static pure oxygen at 200 torr
Specimen geometry: as-cast disc, ~7 mm diameter, 1 mm thickness
Quenching after oxidation for phase confirmation

key_sections.name: Introduction
key_sections.purpose: Highlights the need for low-Cr oxidation-resistant alloys, reviews limited knowledge on Fe–Mn–Al oxidation, and defines the aim of identifying composition-dependent scale morphologies.

key_sections.name: Experimental Procedure
key_sections.purpose: Describes alloy preparation, specimen geometry, oxidation conditions (temperature, time, oxygen pressure), weight-gain measurement, and characterization techniques (SEM, EDAX, metallography, X-ray, EPMA).

key_sections.name: Results: Oxidation of Fe–Mn–Al Alloys (24 h and 100 h at 600–1000°C), Alloys Containing Chromium
key_sections.purpose: Presents oxidation kinetics data, weight gains as functions of Mn and Al, the 800°C oxide map with four morphological scale regions (I–IV), and the effect of chromium additions.

key_sections.name: Discussion: Regions I–IV and Chromium-Containing Alloys
key_sections.purpose: Interprets the scale structures in each composition region, explains austenite-induced breakdown, nodule growth mechanism in six stages, and compares ternary and quaternary alloy behavior.

key_sections.name: Conclusions
key_sections.purpose: Summarizes the protective alumina-forming composition range, the detrimental role of austenite, the beneficial effect of chromium in reducing required Al, and the recommendation for fully ferritic alloys.
